description: India's economy shows resilience despite global turbulence, with an interim Iran-US peace deal potentially boosting supply chains and easing geopolitical pressures, according to the RBI's Financial Stability Report. While macroeconomic fundamentals are strong, the nation remains vulnerable to imported energy price shocks. The report highlights sustained economic activity but warns of inflation risks from supply disruptions and potential fiscal deficit pressures.

Meta on contractors posing as teens to ask rival chatbots 'sensitive' questions
description: Meta has defended its contractors posing as teenagers to test rival AI chatbots on sensitive topics like suicide and drugs. The company claims this is standard practice for ensuring AI safety and age-appropriateness, though rivals like OpenAI, Google, and Character. AI deny authorizing such testing. The project reportedly involved thousands of prompts and images, raising questions about data usage and AI safety protocols.

title: Her son was diagnosed with autism at 2. What she did next is a lesson in parenting
description: A Mumbai mother defied dire predictions after her son Vihaan's autism diagnosis, refusing to let it define his potential.  Despite early struggles with communication and societal judgment, Sayuri Dalvi's unwavering support and focus on shared activities like running helped Vihaan thrive. Now 21, he drives, writes, and inspires thousands, proving that limitations are often societal, not inherent.

Ketan death case: Lohagad Fort closed for visitors as Lonavala police intensify probe
description: Lohagad Fort was temporarily closed Tuesday as police investigate the Ketan Agarwal murder. Accused Chetan Chaudhary will undergo forensic gait analysis to verify his presence at the fort on the day of the incident. Investigators also plan a fresh crime scene reconstruction with Chaudhary and hope to recover his alleged attire. Both Chaudhary and co-accused Siya Goyal are in police custody until July 3.

40°C and counting: Europe's heatwave exposes a continent built for cold
description: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. Buildings that sheltered against frosty temperatures have turned into sweltering traps, revealing a serious flaw in adapting to intense summer heat. With infrastructure buckling under record temperatures and a surge in heat-related fatalities, a fundamental reevaluation of urban planning is desperately needed to face an increasingly hotter reality.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has exited New York City real estate at a steep loss, selling his stake in struggling landlord A&E Real Estate for just six cents on the dollar. The Bloomberg-reported sale lands as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze on one million rent-stabilized apartments squeezes landlords already battered by 2019 rent laws, soaring costs and mounting unpaid rent.

However, electric vehicle companies surged following Delhi's new EV policy, signaling a boost for clean mobility. While benchmarks faltered, mid- and small-cap segments showed resilience. Analysts suggest the market is in a consolidation phase, with positive long-term prospects for EV adoption despite broader caution.

Reports indicate that the victim had voiced concerns regarding infidelity and was troubled by his fiancée's actions before the fatal incident. Ignoring family warnings about her history due to their connections, the investigation now delves deeper into this suspected murder case.

title: BSNL failed to restore landline; consumer commission orders Rs 15,000 compensation
description: A consumer's fight against BSNL has been vindicated by the Madhya Pradesh State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. The commission upheld an order for BSNL to restore a landline connection and pay Rs 15,000 in compensation. BSNL failed to provide evidence for its claims of cable damage and inability to repair, leading the commission to dismiss their appeal as baseless.

4-year-old boy falls into 220-foot-deep borewell in Haryana; rescue operation under way
description: A four-year-old boy, Nirbhay, tragically fell into a 220-foot-deep borewell in Ambala's Dhanaura village while playing near his father.  Rescue operations involving NDRF, SDRF, and the Army are underway. The incident occurred around 6:30 am when the child was reportedly throwing soil into the open borewell and slipped.  Authorities were alerted after initial rescue attempts by family and locals failed.

title: Panic in central Delhi as massive fire breaks out at Barakhamba Road restaurant
description: On Tuesday afternoon, a fire incident occurred at the Parikrama Restaurant on Barakhamba Road in central Delhi, prompting an urgent reaction from emergency crews. The Delhi Fire Service effectively controlled the fire, ensuring there were no reports of injuries. The initial outbreak took place at approximately 3:40 pm, and further details will be provided as the investigation unfolds.

title: 'I'm very happy': Jadeja reacts to Ben Stokes' shock retirement
description: Former India captain Ajay Jadeja lauded Ben Stokes' retirement, calling it the "right call" and a testament to his refusal to be dictated by external forces. Jadeja admired Stokes' principled stand, suggesting the all-rounder wouldn't tolerate administrators controlling his personal life.  Meanwhile, Sanjay Manjrekar expressed sadness, calling Stokes' departure an "end of an era" and highlighting his match-winning prowess on the big stage.

description: The incident took place near Heritage Pride and was reported to the Mumbai Fire Brigade at 2.58 pm. Fire brigade personnel, police, a 108 ambulance and BMC ward staff rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. Officials said all 15 students were travelling in the Universal High School van when the tree was uprooted and fell on the vehicle.

description: A Madhya Pradesh dental association has suspended its treasurer, Dr. Muskan Soni, for five years after she allegedly justified the murder of a Pune businessman on social media. The association cited "acts of indiscipline" and "highly inappropriate, offensive and disrespectful remarks" as reasons for the severe action. Despite an apology, the organization deemed it insufficient, emphasizing a zero-tolerance policy for conduct undermining ethical standards and professional dignity.

title: Anti-drone arsenal, radars & anti-aircraft guns: How Army is keeping Amarnath pilgrims safe
description: As the Amarnath yatra approaches, the Army has implemented a state-of-the-art security system featuring advanced drone detection technology and anti-aircraft weaponry to address aerial threats. Collaborating with various security agencies, this holistic strategy guarantees ongoing monitoring along pilgrimage routes. This comprehensive security arrangement is designed to protect the thousands of devotees participating in this important annual Hindu event.

Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi finally make his India debut in Durham?
description: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut remains elusive despite strong IPL form, as the team suffered a shock T20I series loss to Ireland. With top-order struggles persisting, the upcoming England series presents a potential opportunity for the young batting star. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate confirmed Sooryavanshi's readiness but emphasised the need for patience and backing established players.

title: A dad’s visit to women’s washroom with daughters turned controversial after police called
description: A father's decision to take his young daughters into an empty women's restroom at a gas station sparked a viral debate. Tyler Brodsky chose this over the men's room's condition and occupancy. Another man's confrontation and call to police, however, divided parents on child safety versus women's privacy. Police later confirmed Brodsky did nothing wrong, highlighting the difficult choices parents face.

title: 'Nothing to do with India': England cricketer clarifies after accusing ICC of bias
description: England cricketers Kate Cross and Alex Hartley have faced severe backlash, including death threats, after their criticism of the ICC's Women's T20 World Cup semi-final scheduling was misinterpreted. They clarified their comments targeted the tournament's policy, not the Indian team, which was automatically placed in an earlier slot if they qualified. The controversy became moot when India was eliminated, reverting the schedule to the standard format.

title: Rats, cockroaches, rotting food: FDA suspends six Mumbai eateries in hygiene crackdown
Hormuz, Suez, Taiwan Strait: Why the world’s supply chains now need war-room planning
description: Global supply chains face unprecedented threats as maritime chokepoints become geopolitical battlegrounds. The US-Iran conflict highlighted how disruptions in vital waterways like the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb trigger widespread economic fallout, from soaring freight rates to increased inflation. Other critical routes, including the Suez, Taiwan, Malacca, and Panama Canals, are also under scrutiny, demanding a shift towards war-room level planning for future crises.

R. Madhavan left stunned by 3-year-old drummer leading full orchestra
description: Actor R. Madhavan was left in awe by a viral video of Russian child prodigy Lyonya Shilovsky, who led a full orchestra at just three years old. The clip, showcasing the young drummer's exceptional rhythm and confidence, continues to inspire audiences worldwide. Shilovsky, who began drumming at one, became an internet sensation with his remarkable performance of "Can-Can," demonstrating a maturity rarely seen in such a young musician.

title: Tier-II cities to drive affordable housing finance growth through FY28: Report
description: Affordable housing finance companies are poised for strong growth, with assets expected to rise 19-20% annually through FY28. This surge is fueled by demand in smaller cities, improving housing affordability, and small business financing needs. While home loans will see steady expansion, loans against property are projected for faster growth, though lenders remain cautious on smaller ticket sizes due to past stress.

Old video of fake Army Brigadier Aryan Verma motivating UPSC aspirants resurfaces— watch
description: A resurfaced video shows Aryan Verma, arrested for impersonating an Indian Army Brigadier, addressing UPSC aspirants with armed guards. Verma, who claimed to have switched career ambitions from medicine to UPSC after a public event, encouraged interaction with outsiders. The 21-year-old was apprehended with associates after allegedly posing as a senior officer, travelling in a military-marked SUV and possessing fake credentials.

title: Europe heatwave: Indian AC makers eye market, face competition from China, South Korea
description: So far, exports have remained limited, partly because domestic air-conditioner penetration in India is still only around 10-11%. Air conditioners are also the only white goods segment covered under the production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme, prompting the government to push for higher export earnings from the industry.

FIFA World Cup 2026 scripts unprecedented history after Germany, Netherlands crash out
description: A historic World Cup saw two top-10 nations, Germany and the Netherlands, crash out in the opening knockout round. Germany's campaign ended on penalties against Paraguay after a controversial disallowed goal. The Netherlands also fell to Morocco in a dramatic shootout, with Morocco's goalkeeper proving decisive. This marks a significant upset in the tournament's history.

Ukraine strikes Russian satellite communications hub near Moscow again; Russia downs 419 drones
description: Ukraine has successfully targeted Russia's satellite communications center in Dubna for a second time, President Zelenskyy announced. This strike, over 500 kilometers from Ukraine's border, is part of Kyiv's strategy to cripple Russian military intelligence and coordination. Similar facilities in the Moscow and Vladimir regions have also been hit, intensifying Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against key Russian infrastructure.

title: Woman took magnesium daily, then developed an irregular heartbeat
IIT Bombay to set up AI-powered cyber defence hub for insurance with SBI Life
Barrel of backups: India's crude imports bounce back despite Middle East disruptions
description: India's crude oil imports are nearing pre-conflict levels, thanks to refiners diversifying away from traditional Gulf suppliers. Purchases from Russia, the US, and other regions have compensated for Middle East disruptions. Russia remains a favored source due to attractive pricing. While the Strait of Hormuz has reopened, Asian refiners are cautious about immediate increases in Gulf purchases, focusing on existing cargo coverage and maintenance schedules.

title: 'We didn't hold him hostage': Punjab Kings' Shashank Singh breaks silence after FIR against him, father
description: Cricketer Shashank Singh has responded to an FIR alleging assault and illegal confinement by a domestic cook. Singh claims the cook was hired under false pretenses, was taking private photos, and his mother deleted them due to privacy concerns. He denies any physical altercation or holding the cook hostage, stating they considered calling the police themselves after discovering the alleged unauthorized photography.

title: MDMK's Vaiko loses cool over DMK alliance question; supporters try to attack reporters
description: MDMK leader Vaiko lost his composure at a press meet when questioned about his criticism of the DMK. He alleged widespread corruption during the DMK's tenure, particularly in land registration. When pressed on why he hadn't raised these issues earlier, Vaiko cited coalition ethics. The situation escalated with his supporters attempting to confront reporters, but police intervention diffused the tension.

description: A legal battle has erupted over who represents murder accused Siya Goyal. Advocate Ashutosh Srivastava claims his appointment is valid and filed, threatening defamation suits against Siya's brother, Sahil, who denies engaging him. Another lawyer, Vipul Dushing, also claims representation. Siya and co-accused Chetan Chaudhary were remanded in police custody over the alleged murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal.

'994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
description: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
description: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
description: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Ram Temple trust to hold key meet 5 days early, to decide Champat Rai's fate on July 6
description: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has advanced its meeting to July 6, where the resignations of General Secretary Champat Rai and member Anil Mishra are expected to be finalized. The Trust may also discuss operational restructuring for enhanced transparency and consider new members. Discussions will also cover improving visitor facilities, especially during extreme heat, and crowd management strategies.

title: We will hit back: New TNCC chief Tagore sets sight on PM Rahul Gandhi for 2029
